title: Image name is not loaded from oci-dir
body:
### Issue Description

When importing an image from a directory (i.e. `oci-dir`), the name from its `index.json` is not imported, and so can only be referenced by its raw ID.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue (assuming `fedora` is an alias for some image)
```sh
# 1. Pull an image:
  podman pull fedora
# 2. Export that image to a directory (`oci-dir`):
  podman image save fedora -o fedora --format oci-dir
# 3. Remove the image from podman's local repository:
  podman image rm fedora
# 4. Import the saved image from before:
  podman image load -i fedora
# 5. Observe that the image does not have a name:
  podman image ls
```

### Describe the results you received

See “Issue Description.”

### Describe the results you expected

When importing an image from a directory (i.e. `oci-dir`), the name from its `index.json` is imported, like with tarballs.

### podman info output

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Thank you for working on podman!

comments.body:
according to me 
corrections for Reproduction Steps:
1. Use podman save instead of podman image save
2. Use podman load instead of podman image load
3. Use podman images instead of podman image ls
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
